title: pkg/hooks/exec: Add a new package for local hook execution
body:
This wraps [os/exec][0] to:

* Clear the environment when the hook doesn't set `env`.  The runtime spec [has][1]:

    > * env (array of strings, OPTIONAL) with the same semantics as IEEE Std 1003.1-2008's environ.

    And running `execle` or similar with `NULL` `env` results in an empty environment:

    ```console
    $ cat test.c
    #include <unistd.h>

    int main()
    {
      return execle("/usr/bin/env", "env", NULL, NULL);
    }
    $ cc -o test test.c
    $ ./test
    ...no output...
    ```

    Go's [`Cmd.Env`][2], on the other hand, has:

    > If Env is nil, the new process uses the current process's environment.

    This commit works around that by setting `[]string{}` in those cases to avoid leaking the runtime environment into the hooks.

* Roll the `timeout` value (if set) into the passed context.  There's no need for two separate ways to cancel hook execution.

This, like #855, is more groundwork for [calling the poststop hooks ourselves][3] instead of delegating to the OCI runtime.

[0]: https://golang.org/pkg/os/exec/
[1]: https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blob/v1.0.1/config.md#posix-platform-hooks
[2]: https://golang.org/pkg/os/exec/#Cmd
[3]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/730#issuecomment-392959938

comments.body:
Tests are happy, except for `cloud Podman`, whic [has][1]:

```
Running Suite: Libpod Suite
===========================
Random Seed: 1527725677
Will run 286 of 286 specs
Caching alpine...
Caching busybox...
Caching registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest...
Caching docker.io/library/nginx:latest...
"Error reading config blob sha256:ae513a47849c895a155ddfb868d6ba247f60240ec8495482eca74c4a2c13a881: Get https://production.cloudflare.docker.com/registry-v2/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/ae/ae513a47849c895a155ddfb868d6ba247f60240ec8495482eca74c4a2c13a881/data?verify=1527728759-P4yE%2BFI7dYTukwbke06OsUr81Uc%3D: read tcp 10.88.0.11:41116->104.18.124.25:443: read: connection reset by peer"
```

as well as the Python issues discussed in #856.

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/5c0a892f84bc72904f4d2691dd0d7a175e3454e4.2.1527724917027758080/output.log
I've pushed 5c0a892f -> 854a6663 to get `go test -cover ./pkg/hooks/exec` to 100%.

I've pushed 854a6663 -> a492f1d1, rebasing onto master and adding a configurable post-kill timeout for waiting on the killed hook process.  There's also a constant with @mhean's [recommended 10s timeout][1].  One benefit of keeping the timeout configurable is that I can set it to 0s for a test and keep 100% coverage ;).

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/857#discussion_r192194914

Tests are green except for `cloud Podman`, who's [output][1] looks like the networking issues that it's been having trouble with recently (e.g. with #856).

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/a492f1d179071a6729406aa1d19a3d4eff0081e2.2.1527797417223330604/output.log

:pushpin: Commit a492f1d has been approved by `mheon`
